title: 1  Introduction
text:
This is a book created from markdown and executable code.
See Knuth (1984) for additional discussion of literate programming.

What is the derivative of \(f(x) = x^{7} e^{3.6 x}\), evaluated at \(x = 0.73\)?



Using the product rule for \(f(x) = g(x) \cdot h(x)\), where \(g(x) := x^{7}\) and \(h(x) := e^{3.6 x}\), we obtain \[
\begin{aligned}
f'(x) &= [g(x) \cdot h(x)]' = g'(x) \cdot h(x) + g(x) \cdot h'(x) \\
      &= 7 x^{7 - 1} \cdot e^{3.6 x} + x^{7} \cdot e^{3.6 x} \cdot 3.6 \\
      &= e^{3.6 x} \cdot(7 x^6 + 3.6 x^{7}) \\
      &= e^{3.6 x} \cdot x^6 \cdot (7 + 3.6 x).
\end{aligned}
\] Evaluated at \(x = 0.73\), the answer is \[ e^{3.6 \cdot 0.73} \cdot 0.73^6 \cdot (7 + 3.6 \cdot 0.73) = 20.174331. \] Thus, rounded to two digits we have \(f'(0.73) = 20.17\).




What is the derivative of \(f(x) = x^{8} e^{2.3 x}\), evaluated at \(x = 0.58\)?



Using the product rule for \(f(x) = g(x) \cdot h(x)\), where \(g(x) := x^{8}\) and \(h(x) := e^{2.3 x}\), we obtain \[
\begin{aligned}
f'(x) &= [g(x) \cdot h(x)]' = g'(x) \cdot h(x) + g(x) \cdot h'(x) \\
      &= 8 x^{8 - 1} \cdot e^{2.3 x} + x^{8} \cdot e^{2.3 x} \cdot 2.3 \\
      &= e^{2.3 x} \cdot(8 x^7 + 2.3 x^{8}) \\
      &= e^{2.3 x} \cdot x^7 \cdot (8 + 2.3 x).
\end{aligned}
\] Evaluated at \(x = 0.58\), the answer is \[ e^{2.3 \cdot 0.58} \cdot 0.58^7 \cdot (8 + 2.3 \cdot 0.58) = 0.782371. \] Thus, rounded to two digits we have \(f'(0.58) = 0.78\).




What is the derivative of \(f(x) = x^{5} e^{3.1 x}\), evaluated at \(x = 0.78\)?



Using the product rule for \(f(x) = g(x) \cdot h(x)\), where \(g(x) := x^{5}\) and \(h(x) := e^{3.1 x}\), we obtain \[
\begin{aligned}
f'(x) &= [g(x) \cdot h(x)]' = g'(x) \cdot h(x) + g(x) \cdot h'(x) \\
      &= 5 x^{5 - 1} \cdot e^{3.1 x} + x^{5} \cdot e^{3.1 x} \cdot 3.1 \\
      &= e^{3.1 x} \cdot(5 x^4 + 3.1 x^{5}) \\
      &= e^{3.1 x} \cdot x^4 \cdot (5 + 3.1 x).
\end{aligned}
\] Evaluated at \(x = 0.78\), the answer is \[ e^{3.1 \cdot 0.78} \cdot 0.78^4 \cdot (5 + 3.1 \cdot 0.78) = 30.816925. \] Thus, rounded to two digits we have \(f'(0.78) = 30.82\).

Knuth, Donald E. 1984. “Literate Programming.” Comput. J. 27 (2): 97–111. https://doi.org/10.1093/comjnl/27.2.97.

title: 2  Visualization
text:
2.1 Heatmap
Here, we talk about figures.
In summary, this book has no content whatsoever. This is another plain text sentence. This is a bold face sentence.
library(tidyverse)

── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.2.0
✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
✔ ggplot2   4.0.2     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
✔ purrr     1.2.1     
── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors

df = penguins %>% 
  select(species, island) %>% 
  table() %>% 
  as.data.frame

ggplot(data = df) +
  geom_raster(aes(x = species, y = island, fill = Freq)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c()
